steps.title: Spot the contrasting tones
steps.content: Text 1 presents the study as a clear success—”a clear win.” Text 2 acknowledges “encouraging” findings but immediately pivots to introduce concerns: “it's important to consider potential effects on global food supplies.” The key signal is optimism versus caution.

steps.title: Match the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A correctly identifies the difference: Text 1 is optimistic about the environmental benefits (calling them “a clear win”), while Text 2 is cautious despite acknowledging the findings are encouraging (introducing concerns about food supplies).

The other choices misread the texts by claiming Text 2 disputes the percentage, inventing claims about timeframe, or assigning incorrect environmental focuses.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
